rows.definition: polymodel.NewPool (internal/polymodel/polymodel.go) constructs a model-RESIDENCY pool that keeps many prefill-warm models resident under one weight-byte budget, evicting the coldest UNPINNED model (LRU) when an admission would exceed the cap.
rows.distinction: Unlike pool-session's fleet-wide TOKEN-budget Pool (a shared output-token ceiling sessions draw from) or poolprofile-cachemeta's per-tier hardware POOLING profile, this pool bounds resident model WEIGHT BYTES and owns no tokens, KV, or weights — only residency plus LRU recency bookkeeping.

rows.definition: cachemeta.DefaultPoolProfiles returns a representative PoolProfile per residency tier — HBM/DRAM/NUMA-far host-private, CXL the coherent switch-pooled zero-copy tier, disk local, remote reachable-but-copy-only — the order-of-magnitude default an operator overrides with their fabric's measured topology.
rows.distinction: It is the FACTORY that builds the default per-tier profile map, not the PoolProfile struct describing one tier's pooling character (poolprofile-cachemeta) and not the cross-tenant reuse gate (PoolReuseVerdict (cachemeta gate)).

rows.definition: cachemeta.PoolReuseVerdict is the cross-tenant reuse trust GATE for a SHARED pool: it permits a tenant to attend a pooled cell another tenant wrote only when the cell is un-poisoned, declared shareable beyond its agent scope, adjudicated TaintTrusted, and its materialization key matches on every axis.
rows.distinction: It decides WHO may alias a pooled cell (the trust/scope axis), not the per-tier physical pooling character (poolprofile-cachemeta / DefaultPoolProfiles (cachemeta)) and not the fleet-economics arithmetic (PlanFleetReuse).

rows.definition: FleetReuseResult.PooledPrefillTokens (internal/cachemeta) is the prefill-token COMPUTE cost the chosen pool regime pays for one hot prefix shared by N tenants — one prefill under a reachable or fabric-shareable pool, N prefills when the tier is host-private.
rows.distinction: It is the COMPUTE axis (prefill tokens), distinct from its capacity-axis sibling PooledResidentBytes (fleet-reuse capacity), which counts resident bytes held rather than tokens recomputed.

rows.definition: FleetReuseResult.PooledResidentBytes (internal/cachemeta) is the resident-byte CAPACITY cost the chosen pool regime holds for one hot prefix shared by N tenants — ONE copy under a coherent fabric-shareable pool, N copies under a copy-only or host-private tier.
rows.distinction: It is the CAPACITY axis (resident bytes), distinct from its compute-axis sibling PooledPrefillTokens (fleet-reuse compute), which counts prefill tokens paid rather than bytes resident.

rows.definition: compute.PrewarmCandidate.WarmPoolFree is the pollution gate of the tool-latency prewarm admission decision: a bool that is true only when the lowest-eviction-priority warm KV pool has free capacity, so a WarmNow directive never displaces a demand session's resident prefix.
rows.distinction: This flags free capacity in the opportunistic lowest-priority KV WARM pool, not a fleet-wide token-budget pool (pool-session) or a model-residency pool (Pool (polymodel residency)).

rows.definition: FleetProfile.SharedPool (internal/turnbench) is a fleet-BENCHMARK knob: the number of DISTINCT shared reference reads the modeled agent fleet draws from (the shared catalog size), the parameter that makes the cross-agent dedup curve SATURATE as agent count rises.
rows.distinction: It is a synthetic benchmark catalog-size parameter that shapes a simulated workload, not a real memory/cache pool (poolprofile-cachemeta) or a runtime token budget (pool-session).

rows.definition: cmd/cxlpooldemo is a no-model, no-GPU command that proves the value of a SWITCH-POOLED multi-host shared memory tier (CXL.mem) by computing — purely over cachemeta tier/pool profiles — the three-way fleet economics (per-host private vs copy-only pool vs coherent CXL pool) and the cross-tenant trust gate.
rows.distinction: It is the demo DRIVER that exercises the cachemeta policy plane on deterministic profile data, not the policy plane's profile struct (poolprofile-cachemeta) or its reuse gate (PoolReuseVerdict (cachemeta gate)).
